---
title: "Fisheries Integrated Modeling System (FIMS) Testing Summary Report"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
number-sections: true
crossref:
  chapters: true
  fig-title: Figure     
  tbl-title: Table     
  title-delim: .     
format:
   html:
    fig-width: 8
    fig-height: 4
    code-fold: true     
editor: visual

---

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| include: false

required_pkg <- c("DiagrammeR", "here", "knitr")
pkg_to_install <- required_pkg[!(required_pkg %in%
                                   installed.packages()[, "Package"])]
if (length(pkg_to_install)) install.packages(pkg_to_install)
lapply(required_pkg, library, character.only = TRUE)

library(DiagrammeR)
```

# FIMS testing framework {#sec-fims-testing-framework}

FIMS testing framework includes various types of testing to make sure 
that changes to FIMS code are working as expected. The current testing 
frameworks include both functional and non-functional tests. Details of each type of test can be found in the @sec-testing-glossary: Testing glossary.

```{r}
#| label: fig-create-testing-types-diagram
#| fig-cap: Types of FIMS tests.
#| warning: false
#| echo: false

DiagrammeR::mermaid("
graph LR
  1[Testing Types]-->2[Functional Testing]
  1-->3[Non-Functional Testing]
  2-->2-1[Unit Testing]
  2-->2-2[Integration Testing]
  3-->3-1[Run-time Testing]
  3-->3-2[Platform Compatibility Testing]
  3-->3-3[One-off Testing]
 
")

```

# Test summary{#sec-test-summary}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-create-test-coverage-table
#| tbl-cap: Summary of unit and integration tests.
#| warning: false
#| echo: false

ctest_files <- list.files(
        path = here::here("tests", "gtest"), 
        pattern = "test_"
)
rtest_files <- list.files(
        path = here::here("tests", "testthat"),
        pattern = "test-"
)

# library(testthat)
# report <- covr::file_coverage(
#         source_files = here::here("R", "use_module.R"), 
#         test_files = here::here("tests", "testthat", "test-use-module.R"))
#         report
# test_summary_var <- c(
#         "Test Type", 
#         "Language",
#         "Test Excuted", 
#         "Test Passed",
#         "Test Failed",
#         "Code Coverage", 
# )
# test_summary_data <- data.frame(
#         matrix(ncol = length(test_summary_var), 
#                nrow = length(ctest_files)+length(rtest_files))
# )
# colnames(test_summary_data) <- test_summary_var
# knitr::kable(test_summary_data)
# codecov_data <- covr::package_coverage(
#         path = ".", 
#         type = "tests"
# )

```
## Test type and purpose{#sec-test-type-and-purpose}
## Key findings{#sec-key-findings}
## Defects{#sec-defects}

# Testing glossary{#sec-testing-glossary}
## Unit testing
